port.markdownDescription:
TCP port to listen on for events from Vector's socket sink

TCP port where Log10x listens for events sent by Vector's `socket` sink (`mode: tcp`, `encoding.codec: json`, `framing.method: newline_delimited`). Match this against the `address` in your Vector sink. (Default: 9000)

path.markdownDescription:
Unix domain socket path to listen on (Linux/macOS)

When set, Log10x listens on a Unix domain socket instead of TCP — useful on Linux/macOS to avoid TCP overhead. Configure Vector's `socket` sink with `mode: unix` and a matching `path`. Ignored on Windows.

messageField.markdownDescription:
Name of the JSON field carrying the original log line

Name of the field in each Vector record that contains the actual log message text. The default (`message`) matches Vector's convention for the `file`, `kubernetes_logs`, `journald`, and `socket` sources. Set to e.g. `log` if your sources emit the log line under a different key. (Default: message)

host.markdownDescription:
TCP host of the Vector fluent source receiving processed events

Hostname or IP where Vector's `fluent` source is listening for processed events from Log10x. Pairs with `vectorOutputPort`. (Default: 127.0.0.1)

port.markdownDescription:
TCP port of the Vector fluent source receiving processed events

TCP port where Vector's `fluent` source is listening for processed events from Log10x. MUST differ from the port Log10x's own socket input listens on (default `9000`) or the two would collide on the same listener. (Default: 9001)

path.markdownDescription:
Unix domain socket path of the Vector fluent source (Linux/macOS)

Optional Unix domain socket path for Vector's `fluent` source. When set, takes precedence over `vectorOutputHost` / `vectorOutputPort`. Linux/macOS only — ignored on Windows.

encodeType.markdownDescription:
Output format when outputFields are set. Possible values: [json, delimited]

Specifies how the combined output (main event field plus outputFields) is encoded when writing back to Vector. Possible values: - **json**: formats all fields as a JSON object - **delimited**: formats field values separated by the output delimiter Only takes effect when vectorOutputFields is set. (Default: delimited)
